# Smart Materials for Bioengineering and Biomedicine

Smart materials are characterized by the capability to change properties in response to environmental changes or external stimuli. They can be tailored to have controlled interactions with biological systems, desirable biocompatibility and biodegradability. Such materials have been widely explored for applications in bioengineering and biomedicine, including [drug delivery](https://www.nature.com/collections/ceeibeabaa/content/Stimuli-responsive-therapy/drug-delivery), [biosensing](https://www.nature.com/collections/ceeibeabaa/content/Biosensing/bioimaging), [bioimaging](https://www.nature.com/collections/ceeibeabaa/content/Biosensing/bioimaging) and [tissue engineering](https://www.nature.com/collections/ceeibeabaa/content/Tissue-engineering/implants). By tapping the potential of [smart materials](https://www.nature.com/collections/ceeibeabaa/content/Smart-materials-in-medical-devices), we can transform healthcare to be safer, more effective, affordable, and accessible.

This cross-journal Collection between *Nature Communications*, *Communications Biology*, *Communications Engineering*, *Communications Materials*, *Communications Medicine* and *Scientific Reports* brings together the latest developments in smart materials for bioengineering and biomedicine. Topics of interest include, but are not limited to, the following:

**This Collection supports and amplifies research related to SDG 3: Good Health and Well-Being.**

![Nano robot 3D render. Medical concept future. Isolated](https://media.springernature.com/w580h326/nature-cms/uploads/collections/GettyImages-639615402-ec63a3d8912895d8430ff513f31a460d.jpg)

## Stimuli-responsive therapy/drug delivery

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs42003-025-08095-8/MediaObjects/42003_2025_8095_Figa_HTML.png)

### [Amphiphilic mPEG-PLGA copolymer nanoparticles co-delivering colistin and niclosamide to treat colistin-resistant Gram-negative bacteria infections](/articles/s42003-025-08095-8)

The amphiphilic mPEG-PLGA nanoparticles co-loaded with colistin and niclosamide possess great potential in overcoming colistin resistance and can be applied to the treatment of colistin-resistant bacterial infections.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs42003-024-06121-9/MediaObjects/42003_2024_6121_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Between hope and reality: treatment of genetic diseases through nucleic acid-based drugs](/articles/s42003-024-06121-9)

Nucleic acids-based drugs aim to fix the genetic problem at its source and emerge as a promising new class of drugs. Recent advances in this field enabled their approval for the treatment of orphan genetic diseases for which no cure was available.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-024-46328-2/MediaObjects/41467_2024_46328_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Endogenous stimuli-responsive separating microneedles to inhibit hypertrophic scar through remodeling the pathological microenvironment](/articles/s41467-024-46328-2)

The treatment of hypertrophic scar (HS) is hindered by the low bioavailability of drugs and the pathological microenvironment. Here the authors report a separating microneedle drug delivery system responsive to high reactive oxygen species levels and overexpression of matrix metalloproteinases to remodel the pathological microenvironment for HS treatment.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-024-46245-4/MediaObjects/41467_2024_46245_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Janus microparticles-based targeted and spatially-controlled piezoelectric neural stimulation via low-intensity focused ultrasound](/articles/s41467-024-46245-4)

Wireless and localized stimulation of neural cells remains challenging. Here, the authors propose piezoelectric magnetic Janus microparticles that can target and stimulate neurons under low-intensity ultrasound through voltage-gated ion channels.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-024-44963-3/MediaObjects/41467_2024_44963_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Redox-responsive polymer micelles co-encapsulating immune checkpoint inhibitors and chemotherapeutic agents for glioblastoma therapy](/articles/s41467-024-44963-3)

Immune checkpoint blockade-based immunotherapy has shown limited efficacy in patients with glioblastoma (GBM). Here the authors describe the design of redox-responsive micelles for increasing the delivery of paclitaxel and anti-PD-L1 in the brain, showing improved anti-tumor immune response in preclinical GBM models.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-44564-6/MediaObjects/41467_2023_44564_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Programmable adhesion and morphing of protein hydrogels for underwater robots](/articles/s41467-023-44564-6)

Soft robots have potential in carrying out underwater tasks, but achieving the right level of adhesion and shape-changing ability is challenging. Here, the authors report the development of protein-based hydrogels with iron oxide nanoparticles with photothermal and magnetic responsiveness, capable of carrying out complex tasks.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs42003-023-05643-y/MediaObjects/42003_2023_5643_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Enzyme-controlled, nutritive hydrogel for mesenchymal stromal cell survival and paracrine functions](/articles/s42003-023-05643-y)

A nutritive hydrogel enhances the survival of human mesenchymal stromal cells by providing physiological glucose levels in a controlled manner, eliciting new blood vessel formation in vivo.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-43571-x/MediaObjects/41467_2023_43571_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [A self-stabilized and water-responsive deliverable coenzyme-based polymer binary elastomer adhesive patch for treating oral ulcer](/articles/s41467-023-43571-x)

The therapeutic benefits of biomaterials-based treatments for oral ulcer have been limited by the materials’ poor adhesion and short-time retention in oral cavity. Here, the authors report a polymer binary elastomer adhesive patch that allows water-responsive sustainable delivery of bioactive small molecules and durable adhesion to oral mucosal wounds to achieve efficient therapy of oral ulcer.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-43067-8/MediaObjects/41467_2023_43067_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Biofilm microenvironment triggered self-enhancing photodynamic immunomodulatory microneedle for diabetic wound therapy](/articles/s41467-023-43067-8)

The treatment of diabetic wounds tends to be hindered by complex wound environments, and the critical role of the microenvironment in the chronic diabetic wounds has not been explored for therapeutic development. Here, the authors develop a wound microenvironment-responsive microneedle bandage to achieve self-enhanced, catabolic and dynamic therapy of chronic wounds.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-43070-z/MediaObjects/41467_2023_43070_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Pathogenesis-adaptive polydopamine nanosystem for sequential therapy of ischemic stroke](/articles/s41467-023-43070-z)

Reperfusion is a main strategy for restoring blood supply after ischemic stroke, but it induces neuroinflammation that undergoes dynamic progression, hindering the treatment of ischemic stroke. Here, the authors report a pathogenesis-adaptive nanosystem for sequential and on-demand regulation of reperfusion-induced dynamic neuroinflammation for ischemic stroke therapy.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-40954-y/MediaObjects/41467_2023_40954_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Self-triggered thermoelectric nanoheterojunction for cancer catalytic and immunotherapy](/articles/s41467-023-40954-y)

The exogenous excitation requirement and electron-hole pair recombination are the key factors limiting the application of catalytic therapies. Here, the authors address these limitations by designing a tumor microenvironment-specific self-triggered thermoelectric nanoheterojunction with a self-built-in electric field that facilitates charge separation for cancer treatment.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-39129-6/MediaObjects/41467_2023_39129_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Scarless wound healing programmed by core-shell microneedles](/articles/s41467-023-39129-6)

Effective reprogramming of chronic wound healing remains challenging due to the limited drug delivery efficacy hindered by physiological barriers, as well as the inappropriate dosing timing in distinct healing stages. Here, the authors report a core-shell structured microneedle array patch with programmed functions which dynamically modulates the wound immune microenvironment according to the varied healing phases

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs43246-023-00411-1/MediaObjects/43246_2023_411_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Design and evaluation of multi-core raspberry-like platinum nanoparticles for enhanced photothermal treatment](/articles/s43246-023-00411-1)

Platinum nanoparticles are promising candidates for enhancing radiotherapy sensitivity. Here, platinum-based nanomaterials with a multi-core structure show efficient near-infrared photothermal treatment on glioblastoma tumoroids with good biostability.

## Biosensing/bioimaging

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs43246-024-00511-6/MediaObjects/43246_2024_511_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Skin-interfacing wearable biosensors for smart health monitoring of infants and neonates](/articles/s43246-024-00511-6)

Wearable sensors have been widely studied, but research has tended to focus on their use in adults. This Review explores skin-interfacing smart health systems that are designed with infants and neonates in mind.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-40747-3/MediaObjects/41467_2023_40747_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Simultaneous magnetic resonance imaging of pH, perfusion and renal filtration using hyperpolarized 13C-labelled Z-OMPD](/articles/s41467-023-40747-3)

pH alterations are a hallmark of many pathologies including cancer and kidney disease. Here the authors describe [1,5- 13 C2]Z-OMPD as a probe for hyperpolarized 13C-MRI with good pH sensitivity and hyperpolarization properties which combined with tailored MRI protocols allow sub-minute imaging of pH, renal perfusion and filtration simultaneously.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs42003-024-06164-y/MediaObjects/42003_2024_6164_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Post-click labeling enables highly accurate single cell analyses of glucose uptake ex vivo and in vivo](/articles/s42003-024-06164-y)

For accurate single cell analyses of glucose uptake ex vivo and in vivo, the authors developed a method using glucose transporter-dependent import of clickable sugars, coupled with fluorescent post-labeling and multicolor flow cytometry.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs44172-024-00194-4/MediaObjects/44172_2024_194_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Radar near-field sensing using metasurface for biomedical applications](/articles/s44172-024-00194-4)

Mohammad Omid Bagheri and colleagues introduce a metasurface-enhanced millimetre-wave radar system designed for near-field biosensing. Their device adapts to the properties of the skin-device interface, providing heightened diagnostic precision in wearable healthcare monitoring applications.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-024-46904-6/MediaObjects/41467_2024_46904_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Photoswitchable polyynes for multiplexed stimulated Raman scattering microscopy with reversible light control](/articles/s41467-024-46904-6)

Bioimaging with photocontrol and multiplexing capability is vital for studying cellular interactions and dynamics, but multiplexed stimulated Raman scattering (SRS) imaging with reversible photocontrol is elusive. Here, the authors report SRS microscopy with Carbow-switch enabling multiplexed SRS imaging and tracking in live cells with reversible photocontrol and high spatiotemporal selectivity.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-024-46668-z/MediaObjects/41467_2024_46668_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Photooxidation triggered ultralong afterglow in carbon nanodots](/articles/s41467-024-46668-z)

Biocompatible afterglow materials have potential in imaging applications, but are challenging to prepare. Here the authors report the development of carbon nanodots with near-infrared afterglow, and demonstrate their use in imaging for tumour resection.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs43246-024-00468-6/MediaObjects/43246_2024_468_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Interstitial fluid-based wearable biosensors for minimally invasive healthcare and biomedical applications](/articles/s43246-024-00468-6)

There is growing interest in biosensors that detect biomarkers in interstitial fluid. This Review provides an overview of recent developments on interstitial fluid biosensors, including device fabrication and sensing mechanisms, and outstanding challenges faced.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs42003-024-05965-5/MediaObjects/42003_2024_5965_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [EGFR signaling and pharmacology in oncology revealed with innovative BRET-based biosensors](/articles/s42003-024-05965-5)

BRET-based biosensors allow the quantitative live-cell monitoring of RTKmediated signaling, revealing ligand-specific spatiotemporal and mutation-specific signaling biases, advancing the understanding of RTK functional selectivity.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs42003-024-05806-5/MediaObjects/42003_2024_5806_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [M13 phage grafted with peptide motifs as a tool to detect amyloid-β oligomers in brain tissue](/articles/s42003-024-05806-5)

Bacteriophages displaying amyloidogenic peptides are able to identify amyloid-beta oligomers in *post-mortem* brain tissue of APP/PS1-transgenic mice and of Alzheimer patients.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-43090-9/MediaObjects/41467_2023_43090_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Imaging cellular forces with photonic crystals](/articles/s41467-023-43090-9)

Current techniques for visualizing cell generated forces suffer from throughput limitations. Here, Gu et al. introduced photonic crystal cellular force microscopy, inspired by chameleons, enabling visualization and quantification of vertically directed cell forces, well-suited for drug screening.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-022-35074-y/MediaObjects/41467_2022_35074_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [A self-powered ingestible wireless biosensing system for real-time in situ monitoring of gastrointestinal tract metabolites](/articles/s41467-022-35074-y)

Metabolic dynamics within the small intestine are difficult to study due to the lack of in situ access. Here, the authors report an ingestible, self-powered, and wireless biosensing system, demonstrating proof-of-principle real-time glucose monitoring in the small intestines of pigs.

## Tissue engineering/implants

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs43246-025-00790-7/MediaObjects/43246_2025_790_Figa_HTML.png)

### [Bilayer sol-gel system for local prevention in prosthetic joint infections and osteointegration improvement](/articles/s43246-025-00790-7)

Prosthetic joint infection is a severe complication of arthroplasties, requiring effective prevention and osteoinduction strategies. Here, a bilayer coating system with antibiotics for infection prevention and dipyridamole for osteointegration showed significant infection reduction and enhanced bone mineralization.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs42003-025-07741-5/MediaObjects/42003_2025_7741_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [The role of the glutathione pathway in tracheal regeneration with aortic allografts through antioxidant-driven tissue integration](/articles/s42003-025-07741-5)

This study elucidates the role of the glutathione pathway in tracheal regeneration with aortic allografts, demonstrating its antioxidant-driven modulation of immune responses, extracellular matrix remodeling, and epithelial integration, advancing airway tissue engineering.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-024-45938-0/MediaObjects/41467_2024_45938_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [4D printed hydrogel scaffold with swelling-stiffening properties and programmable deformation for minimally invasive implantation](/articles/s41467-024-45938-0)

3D printing has potential in designing personalised scaffolds, but minimally invasive implantation is still challenging. Here, the authors report the development of a polyurethane material with temperature triggered shape memory and water triggered deformation that allows for transcatheter delivery.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs43246-024-00503-6/MediaObjects/43246_2024_503_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [3D printed titanium carbide MXene-coated polycaprolactone scaffolds for guided neuronal growth and photothermal stimulation](/articles/s43246-024-00503-6)

Neural circuitry is important for comprehending computational mechanisms and physiology of the brain but controlling neuronal connectivity and response in 3D is challenging. Here, titanium carbide MXene-coated 3D polycaprolactone scaffolds are demonstrated to effectively control neuronal interconnection.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs43246-024-00462-y/MediaObjects/43246_2024_462_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Durable plasma-mediated zwitterionic grafting on polymeric surfaces for implantable medical devices](/articles/s43246-024-00462-y)

Poor material biocompatibility of implanted medical devices endangers patient safety and impairs device functionality. Here, durable zwitterion grafts attached onto polymeric surfaces via plasma functionalization lead to superhydrophilic materials for safer and more durable devices.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-024-48417-8/MediaObjects/41467_2024_48417_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Mechanically robust and personalized silk fibroin-magnesium composite scaffolds with water-responsive shape-memory for irregular bone regeneration](/articles/s41467-024-48417-8)

The regeneration of critical-size bone defects, especially those with irregular shapes, remains a clinical challenge. Here, the authors report a shape-memory, tailorable, self-adaptive and bioactive silk fibroin/magnesium composite scaffold that can quickly match irregular defects by simple trimming and lead to good interface integration.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-43421-w/MediaObjects/41467_2023_43421_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Four-dimensional hydrogel dressing adaptable to the urethral microenvironment for scarless urethral reconstruction](/articles/s41467-023-43421-w)

Urethral repair can be carried out using hydrogels, but the harsh microenvironment hinders the repair. Here, the authors report the development of a 4D hydrogel dressing that can provide an early-vascularised and later-antifibrogenic microenvironment to assist in scarless reconstruction.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-42008-9/MediaObjects/41467_2023_42008_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Chronological adhesive cardiac patch for synchronous mechanophysiological monitoring and electrocoupling therapy](/articles/s41467-023-42008-9)

Flexible electronic hydrogels that allow conformal tissue integration, online precision diagnosis, and simultaneous tissue regeneration are desired for advancing the treatment of myocardial infarction. Here, the authors report a chronological adhesive hydrogel patch integrating diagnostic and therapeutic functions through mechanophysiological monitoring and electrocoupling therapy.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-41037-8/MediaObjects/41467_2023_41037_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Targeted mechanical stimulation via magnetic nanoparticles guides in vitro tissue development](/articles/s41467-023-41037-8)

Highly localized mechanical forces that shape in vivo tissue development remain challenging to recapitulate in vitro. Here the authors use magnetically actuated nanoparticles to generate spatially defined forces within organoids, which guide the spatial organization of tissue patterning and growth.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-022-35440-w/MediaObjects/41467_2022_35440_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Temperature-adaptive hydrogel optical waveguide with soft tissue-affinity for thermal regulated interventional photomedicine](/articles/s41467-022-35440-w)

Light penetration and overheating are major issues facing the application of photothermal therapy. Here, the authors develop a temperature responsive hydrogel optical waveguide for controlled delivery of light to deep tumours and demonstrate biocompatibility and temperature responsive phototherapy in vivo

## Smart materials in medical devices

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41598-025-93209-9/MediaObjects/41598_2025_93209_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Bidirectional rotational antagonistic shape memory alloy actuators for high-frequency artificial muscles](/articles/s41598-025-93209-9)

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs44172-024-00328-8/MediaObjects/44172_2024_328_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Implantable silicon neural probes with nanophotonic phased arrays for single-lobe beam steering](/articles/s44172-024-00328-8)

When mapping brain activity with optogenetic techniques, patterned illumination is critical for targeted stimulation. Here, implantable silicon neural probes forming a single steerable beam are developed and in vivo demonstrations reported the device’s potential for deep brain optogenetic stimulation

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-024-47718-2/MediaObjects/41467_2024_47718_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Autonomous self-healing supramolecular polymer transistors for skin electronics](/articles/s41467-024-47718-2)

Integrating self-healing capabilities into skin-like stretchable transistors presents a persistent challenge. Here, by using a supramolecular polymer matrix, the authors develop autonomous self-healing transistors and skin-like logic circuits.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-024-47074-1/MediaObjects/41467_2024_47074_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Flexible large-area ultrasound arrays for medical applications made using embossed polymer structures](/articles/s41467-024-47074-1)

Current ultrasound transducers are bulky and rigid. Here, the authors describe a new way to realize large-area and mechanically flexible ultrasound arrays on polymer foils suited for wearable ultrasound applications

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs43246-024-00469-5/MediaObjects/43246_2024_469_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [A conducting polymer-based array with multiplex sensing and drug delivery capabilities for smart bandages](/articles/s43246-024-00469-5)

Smart devices for wound management combine biosensing with drug release. Here, a smart theranostic bandage is reported that can detect pH and uric acid levels and release antibiotics as necessary, all of which can be done remotely.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-42184-8/MediaObjects/41467_2023_42184_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Stretchable piezoelectric biocrystal thin films](/articles/s41467-023-42184-8)

Developing piezoelectric biocrystals that are stretchable while maintaining structure and stable piezoelectricity is challenging. Here, Li et al. report an amino acid-based piezoelectric biocrystal with omnidirectional stretchability enabled by a truss-like network, for wearable and implantable devices.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-42214-5/MediaObjects/41467_2023_42214_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Self-protection soft fluidic robots with rapid large-area self-healing capabilities](/articles/s41467-023-42214-5)

The soft fluidic robots designed so far are lacking intelligent self-protection and present poor fluidic power source. Here, the authors report soft fluidic robots that integrate soft electrohydrodynamic pumps, healing electrofluids, actuators and E-skins endowing them with self-sensing, self-judgment, and self-heating behaviours for rapid self-healing.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-38689-x/MediaObjects/41467_2023_38689_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Pangolin-inspired untethered magnetic robot for on-demand biomedical heating applications](/articles/s41467-023-38689-x)

Untethered soft robots developed to date display limited functionalities beyond locomotion and cargo delivery. Here, the authors present a pangolin-inspired robotic design which enables heating > 70 °C at distances > 5 cm without compromising their compliance, for biomedical applications.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-39315-6/MediaObjects/41467_2023_39315_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Snowflake-inspired and blink-driven flexible piezoelectric contact lenses for effective corneal injury repair](/articles/s41467-023-39315-6)

The cornea is susceptible to various injuries with a complicated repair process. Here, the authors propose a snowflake-inspired, blink-driven flexible piezoelectric contact lens for corneal injury repair.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-38040-4/MediaObjects/41467_2023_38040_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Ultra-stretchable and biodegradable elastomers for soft, transient electronics](/articles/s41467-023-38040-4)

Stretchable and degradable elastomers are crucial for developing transient and bioresorbable electronics. Herein, Han et al. tuned the diverse properties of biodegradable PLCL elastomers and demonstrated their application in soft, perceptive robotic grippers and transient, suture-free cardiac jackets.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs41467-023-36690-y/MediaObjects/41467_2023_36690_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Responsive materials and mechanisms as thermal safety systems for skin-interfaced electronic devices](/articles/s41467-023-36690-y)

Minimizing patient risk to thermal failure in wearable electronics typically requires complex circuit control, sensors, or passive materials. Here, Yoo et al. present an active materials system with a self-inflating bladder that delaminates upon excessive heat, mitigating the risk of thermal injury.

![](https://media.springernature.com/w290h158/springer-static/image/art%3A10.1038%2Fs42003-022-04390-w/MediaObjects/42003_2022_4390_Fig1_HTML.png)

### [Neuroprosthetics: from sensorimotor to cognitive disorders](/articles/s42003-022-04390-w)

This state-of-the-art Review highlights the evolution of neuromodulation and neuroprosthetic systems from sensorimotor to cognitive disorders.

enriched_points.expanded_content: Smart materials actuators enhance the functionality of minimally invasive devices, allowing precise control and adaptation to physiological conditions, thus improving patient outcomes and reducing recovery times.

Shape memory alloys are utilized in stents to enable expansion at body temperature, providing effective support for blood vessels and reducing complications associated with traditional stenting methods.
Emerging trends include the integration of smart materials in personalized medicine, targeted drug delivery systems, and advanced biosensors, aimed at enhancing treatment efficacy and patient safety.
